article_title: Wurzburg team confirms 40-year-old Kardar-Parisi-Zhang growth law in two dimensions using exciton-polariton condensates
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Wurzburg team confirms 40-year-old Kardar-Parisi-Zhang growth law in two dimensions using exciton-polariton condensates
content_preview.summary: Physicists at the University of Wurzburg used a gallium arsenide quantum film cooled to -269.15 C to measure KPZ scaling exponents in 2D, four years after the 1D version was confirmed.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team led by the University of Wurzburg has produced the first experimental demonstration of Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) universal scaling in two dimensions, closing a forty-year gap between a celebrated piece of statistical physics and the laboratory. The result, reported by [ScienceDaily](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260505234622.htm) and [Phys.org](https://phys.org/news/2026-04-universal-surface-growth-law-dimensions.html), was published in *Science* on April 9, 2...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Briefing-grade News piece (589 words). Clear chronology (1986 KPZ, 2015 Diehl theory, 2022 1D Paris confirmation, 2026 2D Würzburg result), accurate technical framing, neutral tone.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read source-0 (ScienceDaily, 200 OK), source-2 (SciTechDaily, 200 OK), source-3 (arXiv 2506.15521, 200 OK) from local snapshots. Phys.org (source-1) returned 403 to chief:review fetcher — bot-block, not archived. All factual claims (1986, GaAs, -269.15°C, 20 micrometer sample, Siddhartha Dam at Würzburg-Dresden ctd.qmat, Sebastian Diehl at University of Cologne, 2015 theoretical groundwork, molecular beam epitaxy, 2022 Paris 1D, DOI 10.1126/science.aeb4154 vol 392 page 221) confirmed verbatim in ScienceDaily and SciTechDaily snapshots. Direct Dam quotes ('When surfaces grow...', 'We have now succeeded in controlling...') and Diehl quote ('highlights just how fundamental this equation is...') verified verbatim across both ScienceDaily and SciTechDaily snapshots. arXiv abstract quote 'reveal correlation dynamics and scaling exponents in excellent agreement with 2D KPZ predictions' verified verbatim in arXiv snapshot.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All headline, summary, and lead claims fully sourced. Multi-source corroboration for every major specific.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, professional, no editorializing or AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No archive collision. Earlier KPZ-related coverage absent from src/content/articles/.
concerns: Article puts quote marks around 'establishes exciton-polariton condensates as a platform for studying nonequilibrium quantum systems' (attributed to arXiv preprint). The actual arXiv abstract reads: 'establish exciton-polariton condensates as a robust experimental platform for exploring 2D nonequilibrium universality quantitatively'. Same substance, but the quoted text is a paraphrase, not a verbatim reproduction. Single corrections note can honestly inform readers.

Phys.org quote attributions ('Diehl told Phys.org', 'said in Phys.org's coverage') are unverifiable from the local snapshot because Phys.org bot-blocked the fetcher (403). The exact same quotes are verified verbatim in ScienceDaily and SciTechDaily, so the substance is sound — but the specific attribution to Phys.org cannot be independently confirmed.
recommendations: File a corrections record noting the paraphrase-in-quotes for the arXiv 'platform for studying nonequilibrium quantum systems' line.
Future submissions: avoid attaching quote marks to paraphrased text from primary-source abstracts; either reproduce verbatim or remove quote marks.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively a clean, well-sourced physics-news article. Single recoverable issue (paraphrase in quote marks attributed to arXiv) is squarely in APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS territory: the substance is fine and a corrections note can honestly inform readers.
